name: copilot-extension
description: Companion extension for copilot-instructions-template — profile management, extension sync, and MCP lifecycle tools for GitHub Copilot agents.

asafelobotomy.profileName.description: Name of the active VS Code profile for this workspace. Set per-profile to enable profile detection by Copilot agents.

asafelobotomy.knownProfiles.description: List of known VS Code profile names. Populated automatically when ensure_repo_profile is called.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_get_active_profile
languageModelTools.userDescription: Detect the active VS Code profile in the current window.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the name of the active VS Code profile for the current workspace. Uses the asafelobotomy.profileName setting. Returns empty string if not configured.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_list_profiles
languageModelTools.userDescription: List all known VS Code profiles from configuration.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns an array of profile names configured in the asafelobotomy.knownProfiles setting. Profiles are registered when ensure_repo_profile is called.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_get_workspace_profile_association
languageModelTools.userDescription: Check if this workspace has a profile binding.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the profile name associated with the current workspace, or null if no association exists. Reads from asafelobotomy.profileName in workspace settings.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_ensure_repo_profile
languageModelTools.userDescription: Create and/or switch to a repo-specific VS Code profile.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Opens the current workspace in a named VS Code profile using 'code . --profile <name>'. If the profile does not exist, VS Code creates it automatically. Sets asafelobotomy.profileName in workspace settings.

profileName.description: The profile name to create or switch to. Convention: use the repo or folder name.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_get_installed_extensions
languageModelTools.userDescription: List all installed VS Code extensions with version info.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns an array of installed extensions with id and version. Uses the VS Code extensions API for accurate profile-aware enumeration.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_sync_extensions_with_recommendations
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Compares installed extensions against .vscode/extensions.json. Returns three lists: missing (recommended but not installed), extra (installed but not recommended), and matched (both installed and recommended).

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_get_mcp_status
languageModelTools.userDescription: Check the status of all configured MCP servers.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads .vscode/mcp.json and reports each configured MCP server with its type (stdio/http), command or URL, args, env keys, enabled/disabled state, and sandbox config. Returns counts of enabled vs disabled servers. Use this for a quick overview of all MCP servers.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_get_mcp_server_config
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get the full configuration of a specific MCP server.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the complete configuration for a named MCP server from .vscode/mcp.json, including type, command, args, env, sandbox settings, and disabled state. Use when you need the full details of one server.

serverName.description: The name of the MCP server as defined in .vscode/mcp.json (e.g. 'filesystem', 'git', 'heartbeat').

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_toggle_mcp_server
languageModelTools.userDescription: Enable or disable an MCP server in .vscode/mcp.json.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sets or removes the 'disabled' flag on a named MCP server in .vscode/mcp.json. Use enabled=true to enable (removes disabled flag) or enabled=false to disable (sets disabled=true). The server will need to be restarted for changes to take effect.

serverName.description: The name of the MCP server to toggle.

enabled.description: true to enable the server, false to disable it.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_restart_mcp_server
languageModelTools.userDescription: Trigger a restart of MCP servers by re-resolving definitions.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Fires the onDidChangeMcpServerDefinitions event, causing VS Code to re-resolve all MCP server definitions from this provider. This effectively restarts servers whose definitions are managed by this extension. Use when an MCP server appears to be unresponsive.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_add_mcp_server
languageModelTools.userDescription: Add a new MCP server to .vscode/mcp.json.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Adds a new MCP server entry to .vscode/mcp.json. For stdio servers, provide command and args. For http servers, provide url. Fails if a server with that name already exists. The file is written with tab indentation to match VS Code defaults.

serverName.description: Unique name for the new MCP server.

type.description: Server transport type: 'stdio' for local process, 'http' for remote HTTP/SSE endpoint.

command.description: For stdio servers: the command to run (e.g. 'npx', 'uvx', 'node').

url.description: For http servers: the MCP endpoint URL.

env.description: Optional environment variables as key-value pairs.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_remove_mcp_server
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Removes a named server entry from .vscode/mcp.json. This is destructive and cannot be undone. Use toggle_mcp_server to disable a server without removing it.

serverName.description: The name of the MCP server to remove.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_get_workspace_info
languageModelTools.userDescription: Detect OS, project manifest, git state, and runtime versions for the current workspace.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns environment info (OS, distro, arch, package manager), project metadata (name, version, language from package.json/pyproject.toml/Cargo.toml/go.mod), git state (branch, commit), runtime versions (node, python), and VS Code context (version, appHost). Use at session start or when you need environment context.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_get_workspace_state
languageModelTools.userDescription: Read the current session state, recent heartbeat events, and HEARTBEAT.md summary.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads .copilot/workspace/runtime/state.json (session state), .heartbeat-events.jsonl (last 20 events), session sentinel status, and HEARTBEAT.md first line. Returns the full state object, recent events array, session active flag, heartbeat summary, and file paths. Use to check session health or debug heartbeat issues.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_session_reflect
languageModelTools.userDescription: Reflect on the current coding session with metrics and personalised prompts.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns session magnitude (small/medium/large), metrics (active minutes, files changed, edits, compactions, duration), reflection prompts (scope check, compaction, test coverage, SOUL values, USER attributes), workspace identity file existence, and a heartbeat record template. Call on significant sessions (≥8 files or ≥30min active). Process silently — update identity files if warranted, surface only actionable findings.

languageModelTools.name: asafelobotomy_spatial_status
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get a compact workspace navigation snapshot with vocabulary, diaries, and session clock.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the workspace vocabulary table from ledger.md, recent diary entries per agent (last 3 bullets each), and a session clock summary (active duration, median session length). Use when you need a quick overview of the workspace state for navigation or context.
